title: SARS-CoV-2 Sequencing on Illumina MiSeq Using ARTIC Protocol: Part 2 - Illumina DNA Prep Protocol

Its purpose is to simplify things for the average state public health laboratory, using equipment and expertise they currently possess, most likely from their funded PulseNet activities.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"evvh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8hptp","text":"Feedback and comments appreciated.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Before You Begin This protocol is an adaptation of the Illumina DNA Prep kit (below). It is laid out specifically for using amplicons from the ARTIC Tiling PCR Protocol. The audience is assumed to be public health laboratorians with access to instruments and reagents used for PulseNet WGS. Four documents are linked below for reference, and can also be found following the link for the Illumina DNA Prep reagents. The consumables and equipment document (CED) should be used to make sure you have the appropriate materials before beginning. There are too many consumables to list out individually in this protocol so please use the document and fill out your Lot# and Exp Dates on this document as well. The Illumina DNA Prep, (M) Tagmentation kit reagents are not listed in the consumables document so they have been added to Table 1 below. For Sample Indices please pay attention to which version you are using as there are several different formats requiring different steps. This protocol is written for 96 Dual Index plate format indices. Your lab will most likely have everything already for their PulseNet activities. The exception to this is PhiX which will be listed in the appropriate step. Illumina DNA Prep Reference Guide Illumina DNA Prep Checklist Illumina DNA Prep Consumable and Equipment (.zip file) Index Adapters Pooling Guide Illumina DNA Prep Tagmentation Kit Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20018704 IDT for Illumina DNA/RNA Sample Indices Illumina, Inc. Catalog #20027213-16 %s %s All reagents except EPM should be brought to room temperature prior to use. EPM should be kept on ice. Check TSB for precipitates. If there are any, heat at 37 °C for 00:10:00 , then vortex until dissolved.

Dilution Plate Preparation Date/Initials:_________________ Prior to starting your DNA prep, samples should be diluted into a 96 well plate as described below. Ideally, you would like each sample to be diluted such that the 30 µL final volume of sample contains 100 ng to 500 ng of DNA. Less than 100 ng of DNA may cause the sample to be under represented in the pool, and modifications to increase the concentration of that sample are not practical when multiplexing the library prep. Much of this protocol can be achieved using 96 well plates, so having specimens organized in columns allows the use of an 8 channel multi-channel pipette.
[ ] Label a 96 well dilution plate with the number of columns required.

[ ] Add enough DNA to reach a total of at least 100 ng ** and add molecular grade water (CED) to bring the total volume to 30 µL . **NOTE: Preferred amount is 100 ng to 500 ng . Less than that can lead to under representation of the sample in the final pool. More is probably ok as long as it is not extreme.
Tagmentation Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses the Bead-Linked Transposomes (BLT) to tagment DNA, which is a process that fragments and tags the DNA with adapter sequences.

[ ] Prepare tagmenation Master Mix using Calculation 1: %s %s **NOTE: Vortex BLT for 10 seconds to ensure proper bead suspension prior to using. Both reagents should be at room temperature before using.
[ ] Vortex master mix, and then add 20 µL to each sample well. Pipette each 10 times to mix.
[ ] Cover and seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED).
[ ] Place the plate in the thermal cycler and run the TAG Program below: 55°C %s %s

Post Tagmentation Clean-up Date/Initials:_________________ This step stops the tagmentation and washes the adapter-tagged DNA on the BLT before PCR amplification. All reagents should be at Room temperature

[ ] Check TSB for precipitates, and then add 10 µL to each tagmentation reaction (sample well). Slowly pipette to mix 10 times to resuspend the beads. If there are precipitates in the TSB then heat at 37 °C for 00:10:00 then vortex until dissolved.
[ ] Seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED), place on the preprogrammed thermal cycler, and run the PTC Program . %s %s
[ ] Place plate on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear.
[ ] Remove and discard supernatant.

[ ] Remove from magnet, and add 100 µL TWB . Pipette to mix**. **NOTE: A deliberately slow pipetting technique minimizes the potential of TWB foaming to avoid incorrect volume aspiration and incomplete mixing.
[ ] Place back on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear. Remove and discard supernatant.
[ ] Repeat TWB washes described above once more, then slowly add 100 µL TWB to the beads. Pipette to mix.

Amplify Tagmented DNA Date/Initials:_________________ This step amplifies the tagmented DNA using a limited-cycle PCR program. The PCR step adds Index 1 (i7) adapters, Index 2 (i5) adapters, and sequences required for sequencing cluster generation. To confirm the indexes selected for low plexity pooling have the appropriate color balance, see the Index Adapters Pooling Guide .
[ ] Prepare PCR master mix using Calculation 2: A B C 1 Reagent Volume (uL) *(#samples+2) 2 EPM 20.0 3 H2O 20.0 Calculation 2: PCR Master Mix
[ ] Vortex and quick spin master mix.
[ ] Remove and discard TWB from samples. Remove from magnet, and immediately add 40 µL PCR master mix, pipette to mix until beads are fully resuspended, then quick spin.

[ ] Add 10 µL indices**, and then pipette to mix. **NOTE: Use the same index strategy that you use for PusleNet organisms. If you are unfamiliar with the PulseNet protocol, see Index Adapters Pooling Guide for guidance on the indexing. A separate Appendix will be created discussing pooling best practices.

[ ] Seal the plate with Microseal 'B' (CED), place on the thermal cycler, and run the BLT PCR Program on the thermal cycler. %s %s **NOTE: This is a 5 cycle PCR reaction designed for the majority of samples in 96 well plate sequencing. If your samples started with less than 100 ng of DNA, or eventually failed for low coverage in your pool, refer to page 10 of the Illumina DNA Prep Reference Guide for guidelines on how to increase the number of cycles for low input samples.
Clean-up Libraries Date/Initials:_________________ This step uses double-sided bead purification procedure to purify the amplified libraries.
[ ] Centrifuge plate at 280 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute , and then place on magnet for 00:05:00 or until clear
[ ] Transfer 45 µL of supernatant to clean wells of a new midi plate (CED), and then remove from magnet
[ ] Vortex and invert SPB until completely resuspended.

[ ] Add 81 µL, (1.8x) of SPB** to samples, pipette 10 times to mix. **NOTE: Since we are using this kit on small amplicons from the ARTIC Protocol, we will follow the DNA Prep instructions for small amplicon clean up, not standard DNA input.
[ ] Seal (CED) plate and incubate for at least 00:05:00 .

[ ] Prepare fresh 80 % volume EtOH using the following calculation: 0.4 mL * (#samples+1) = _______ mL volume EtOH 0.1 mL * (#samples+1) = _______mL volume molecular grade water Add those two volumes together for 80 % volume EtOH.
[ ] Place on magnet for 00:05:00 or until clear. Remove and discard supernatant.

[ ] Perform two 00:00:30 washes with 200 µL 80 % volume EtOH. Remove and discard supernatant after each wash. After second wash make sure to remove residual EtOH with 20 uL pipette. Perform this step on the magnetic stand without disturngin the beads.

[ ] Air dry beads approx. 00:05:00 or until pellet no longer appears shiny and has one or two cracks in it and then remove plate from magnet Note: Avoid allowing pellet to dry for too long as over dry pellets will not efficiently resuspend which may decrease yield.
[ ] Vortex RSB and add 32 µL . Pipette to mix and incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 .
[ ] Place plate on magnet for 00:02:00 . Transfer 30 µL supernatant to new wells.

[ ] Library is now ready for quantification. SAFE STOPPING POINT If you are stopping, seal the plate with Microseal 'B' adhesive or Microseal 'F' foil seal, and store at -25°C to -15°C for up to 30 days.

Qubit Quantification Date/Initials:_________________ It is also recommended to check library quality by fragment analysis using High Sensitivity DNA kits for Agilent TapeStation, Bioanalyzer, or other similar high throughput methodology.
[ ] Prepare Qubit working solution using Calculation 5 . Label assay tubes for samples and standards. %s %s
[ ] Combine 190 µL Qubit working solution + 10 µL Qubit standard into labeled standard tubes
[ ] Combine 198 µL Qubit working solution + 2 µL extracted DNA into labeled tubes
[ ] Vortex all tubes for 2-3 sec and incubate at Room temperature for minimum of 00:02:00 . Read tubes within 01:00:00
[ ] Record sample results.
Denaturation of Pooled Library Date/Initials:_________________ This section demonstrates how to generate a pooled library for V3 reagents on the MiSeq.
[ ] Centrifuge plate at 280 x g, Room temperature, 1 minute

[ ] Make 4 nanomolar (nM) pool using Calculation 6 %s %s Calculation 6: 4nM Pool Pool conc. (Qubit value, ng/µl) Molarity (nM)= (Pool conc. x 1000) / 528 Volume of pool to dilute (µl)= (4 x 50) / Molarity Volume of RSB to dilute (µl)= (50 – Pool to dilute)
[ ] Add 400 µL molecular grade H 2 0 to the 100 µL 1.0 N NaOH aliquot for 0.2 N NaOH
[ ] Combine 5 µL 0.2 N NaOH and 5 µL pooled DNA in a 1.5 mL tube. Incubate 00:05:00 at Room temperature

[ ] Immediately add 990 µL pre-chilled HT1. Pipette to mix, then use Table 2 for desired library pool concentration %s %s Desired final loading concentration □8pM □9pM □10pM □12pM □14pM □15pM □16pM □18pM □20pM Denatured Pooled Library 800µl 900µl 1000µl 600µl 700µl 750µl 800µl 900µl 1000µl HT1 200µl 100µl 0µl 400µl 300µl 250µl 200µl 100µl 0µl
[ ] Place back on magnet 00:03:00 or until clear.
